best_response.text:
TCGA-50-5049

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:                                                                                     

                                                                                         Summary Statement
The right upper lobe demonstrates a 2.8 cm invasive moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma associated with
angiolymphatic and visceral pleural invasion. All sampled lymph nodes are benign. Surgical resection margins are free
of tumor. Pathologic stage is T2N0MX.

PART 1: LUNG, RIGHT UPPER LOBE, BRONCHOSCOPIC BIOPSY -
          RESPIRATORY MUCOSA WITH SUBMUCOSAL SCARRING AND CHRONIC INFLAMMATION.

PART 2: BONE, 5TH RIB, RESECTION -
          PENDING DECALCIFICATION. ADDENDUM REPORT TO FOLLOW.

PART 3: LUNG, RIGHT UPPER LOBE, POSTERIOR SEGMENT, SEGMENTECTOMY -
          A. INVASIVE MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA (2.8 CM IN DIAMETER) ASSOCIATED WITH
              ANGIOLYMPHATIC AND VISCERAL PLEURAL INVASION. MARKED LYMPHOCYTIC HOST RESPONSE.
              PATHOLOGIC STAGE: T2N0MX.
          B. SURGICAL RESECTION MARGINS ARE FREE OF TUMOR.
          C. BACKGROUND LUNG WITH EMPHYSEMATOUS CHANGE.

PART 4: LYMPH NODE, LEVEL 11, BIOPSY -
          THREE (3) FRAGMENTS OF BENIGN LYMPH NODE.

PART 5: LYMPH NODE, LEVEL 4, BIOPSY -
          UNREMARKABLE FIBROADIPOSE TISSUE. NO LYMPHOID TISSUE SEEN.
